name: Compare Models
description: Compare candidate models with scoped objectives, metric selection, evaluation-plan approvals, result analysis, and evidence-backed recommendations.

inputs.description: Candidate model identifiers, checkpoints, endpoints, or model card URLs to compare.
Capability, task, product requirement, or risk the comparison should answer.
Dataset, benchmark, or representative examples for the comparison.
Primary metric or decision criterion for ranking candidates.

permissions.description: Read model cards, dataset cards, benchmark docs, prior results, licenses, and evaluation references.

approval_points.description: Confirm models, objective, task fit, data source, metrics, and decision criteria.
Confirm the bounded evaluation plan, adapters, resource assumptions, and artifact capture before any run starts.
Publish model comparison results, recommendations, or leaderboard-style summaries outside the local workspace.

phases.name: Comparison Scope
phases.objective: Define the evaluation question, candidate set, target users, acceptance criteria, and non-goals.

phases.name: Candidate Intake
phases.objective: Review candidate model metadata, licenses, intended use, compatibility, and dataset assumptions.

phases.name: Metric And Harness
phases.objective: Specify metrics, aggregation, scoring adapters, sample-level outputs, and comparison table schema.

phases.name: Evaluation Plan
phases.objective: Freeze evaluation configuration, environment assumptions, run identifiers, and artifact paths.

phases.name: Results Review
phases.objective: Compare aggregate metrics, baseline deltas, sample-level failures, costs, and qualitative tradeoffs.

phases.name: Recommendation
phases.objective: Produce an evidence-backed recommendation, caveats, and optional publication notes.

required_outputs.description: Objective, candidate models, target users, acceptance criteria, and non-goals.
Candidate model metadata, licenses, intended use, limitations, and source links.
Evaluation dataset source, revision, split mapping, representative examples, and fingerprint.
Metrics, aggregation, tie-breakers, thresholds, and reporting format.
Scoring adapters, task configuration, sample-level result schema, and validation notes.
Evaluation matrix, resource assumptions, run identifiers, and artifact layout.
Runtime, dependency versions, hardware class, and seed controls.
Aggregate model metrics with dataset, split, run id, and ranking fields.
Sample-level predictions, references, scores, and error labels.
Baseline, incumbent, or reference model comparison values.
Recommendation, tradeoffs, caveats, and evidence links.

artifacts.description: Model-by-metric matrix with metadata, scores, costs, and caveats.
Evidence-backed model selection or rejection rationale.

verifiers.description: Comparison objective maps to observable examples, metrics, and a bounded candidate set.
Candidate models, licenses, intended uses, and compatibility constraints are documented.
Evaluation dataset source, revision, split mapping, examples, and fingerprint are recorded.
Metrics, aggregation, thresholds, tie-breakers, and reporting format are documented.
Scoring code or adapter plan has validated fields for a minimal example.
Environment, dependency versions, hardware class, and seed controls are recorded.
Comparison metrics include model id, dataset or split, metric value, and run identifier.
Candidate results are compared against an incumbent, baseline, or reference result.
Sample-level failures, metric caveats, variance, and interpretation risks were reviewed.
Recommendation cites metrics, sample results, candidate metadata, and caveat evidence.
